$comment: Machine-enforced guardrails for the CI-triage agent. These are applied in TypeScript AFTER the model returns its verdict — the model's self-assessment is never trusted for risk gating. This is 'guardrails in code, not in the prompt'.

highRiskPaths.reason: Billing/tax logic. Rate and calculation changes are financial decisions and need human + finance sign-off, even when the code fix is trivial.
Authentication/authorization or secrets. Security must review.
Schema or data migration. Often irreversible; needs DBA review.
